title: Convolvulus malvaeoides
intro: Convolvulus malvaeoides, also known as Egyptian bindweed or morning glory, is a perennial vine native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its twining stems and small, white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Convolvulus malvaeoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
